title: Stellaris® RNA FISH Protocol for Brain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"i4u9","text":"Stellaris® RNA FISH protocol for fresh frozen mouse brain","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3q70j","text":"Storage Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8dn4d","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c876n","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes are shipped dry and can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in this state. Dissolved probe mix should be subjected","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4uuev","text":"to a minimum number of freeze-thaw cycles. For daily and short-term use of dissolved probe mix, storage at +2 to +8 °C in the","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"22f74","text":"dark for up to a month is recommended. For storage lasting longer than a month, we recommend aliquoting and freezing probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6p8so","text":"in the dark at -15 to -30 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4o8u7","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"av6bb","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer should be stored at +2 to +8 °C for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cjat6","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A and Wash Buffer B","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b448r","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffers A and B should be stored at room temperature for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

Wide-field fluorescence microscope (e.g., Nikon Eclipse Ti or equivalent). We provide limited support for confocal applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7c7sh","text":"b) A high numerical aperture (\u003e1.3) and 60-100x oil-immersion objective.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"csipt","text":"c) Strong light source, such as a mercury or metal-halide lamp (newer LED-based light sources may also be sufficient).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6t40o","text":"d) Filter sets appropriate for the fluorophores.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9jf3m","text":"e) Standard cooled CCD camera, ideally optimized for low-light level imaging rather than speed (13 μm pixel size or less is ideal).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9agqo","text":"Preparation of Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bacqf","text":"NOTE: When performing Stellaris RNA FISH, it is imperative to limit RNA degradation. Please ensure that all consumables and","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"afvqb","text":"reagents are RNase-free. Recipes below are for set volumes. Please adjust accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3u4k0","text":"Reconstituting the dried probe stock:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"to45","text":"ShipReady Probe Set (1 nmol): A ShipReady probe set can provide up to 80 hybridizations. Mix well by pipetting up and down, and then vortex and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"994nu","text":"Fixation Buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bous0","text":"Final composition is 3.7% (vol./vol.) formaldehyde in 1X PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"944f3","text":"For a final volume of 50 mL, mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"26m9c","text":"1 mL 20% Formaldehyde solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4b2hp","text":"5 mL 10X Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS), RNase-free","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d47vn","text":"35 mL Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ffm52","text":"TEA Buffer (10X):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"egptc","text":"NOTE: This buffer is light sensitive. steps: Thaw the slide-mounted tissue section to room temperature.
Immerse the slide in cold 4% E.M. grade paraformaldehyde in 1X PBS for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Wash with 1X PBS for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Wash twice with 1X PBS for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Dip the slide in nuclease-free water.
Dip the slide in 1X TEA buffer.

Immerse the slide in 1X TEA + Acetic Anhydride for 10 minutes (Stirring!) *** 00:10:00 Note: Acetic Anhydride must be added fresh each time. Add Acetic Anhydride to fresh 1X TEA buffer after the second 1X PBS wash in step 3, about 1 minute before dipping slides in DEPC water. For 50 mL of 1X TEA, add 63 μL of Acetic Anhydride.
Immerse the slide in 2X SSC for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse the slide in 70% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse the slide in 95% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse the slide in 100% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse the slide in Chloroform for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
3Immerse the slide in 100% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:05:00
Immerse the slide in 95% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Let air dry for 90+ minutes (but no longer than 4 hours). 01:30:00

If frozen before using, warm the reconstituted probe solution to room temperature. Mix well by vortexing, then centrifuge briefly. To prepare the hybridization solution, add 4.0 μL of probe stock solution to 200 μL of hybridization buffer, and then vortex and centrifuge. This creates a working probe solution of 250 nM. This solution will be used on step 18.

Assemble a humidified chamber: 150 mm tissue culture plate; a single water-saturated paper towel placed alongside the inner chamber edge. This chamber will help prevent evaporation of the probe solution from the tissue section.

After slide has dried for 90+ minutes, dispense 200 μL of hybridization buffer containing probe onto the tissue sections of the slide. undetermined Stellaris® RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10

Carefully place a clean 24 x 60 mm rectangular coverglass over the hybridization solution to completely cover the tissue sections and allow for even distribution of the hybridization solution. Place the slide in the humidified chamber, cover with the tissue culture lid, and seal chamber with parafilm.
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for at least 4 hours (incubation can be continued up to 16 hours). 04:00:00

Immerse the slide in wash buffer A, and allow the submerged coverglass to slide off the tissue section. Gentle agitation may be required to remove the coverglass. Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Decant wash buffer A, and then add DAPI nuclear stain (wash buffer consisting of 5 ng/mL DAPI) to counterstain the nuclei.
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Decant DAPI staining buffer, and then immerse slide in Wash Buffer B for 3 minutes. 00:03:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer B Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WB1-20
Immerse slide in 50% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse slide in 85% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Immerse slide in 100% ethanol for 3 minutes. 00:03:00
Let air dry for 5-10 minutes. 00:10:00

Add a drop or two (approximately 50-100 μL) of Prolong Gold Antifade Mountant onto the tissue sections. Cover with a clean 24 x 60 mm coverglass, allowing the antifade to spread evenly across the tissue sections undetermined Prolong Gold Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #P36930
Allow Prolong Gold to cure overnight, in the dark, at room temperature.
Seal the coverglass perimeter with clear nail polish, and allow to dry in the dark.
Proceed to imaging.
